from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triples into a fluent, natural‑language description.
    The implementation groups facts by their subject and builds one
    compound sentence per subject, joining clauses with commas and an
    “and”.  This yields richer, more coherent output and improves BLEU.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper to strip surrounding quotes from literal strings.
    def clean(val: str) -> str:
        return val.strip('\"')

    # Templates that produce a phrase *without* the subject (subject will be added later).
    subj_phrases = {
        "almaMater": lambda o: f"studied at {clean(o)}",
        "birthDate": lambda o: f"was born on {clean(o)}",
        "birthName": lambda o: f"was originally named {clean(o)}",
        "birthPlace": lambda o: f"was born in {clean(o)}",
        "dateOfRetirement": lambda o: f"retired in {clean(o)}",
        "occupation": lambda o: f"was a {clean(o)}",
        "status": lambda o: f"is {clean(o).lower()}",
        "timeInSpace": lambda o: f"spent {clean(o)} minutes in space",
        "mission": lambda o: f"was a crew member of {clean(o)}",
        "selectedByNasa": lambda o: f"was selected by NASA in {clean(o)}",
        "deathDate": lambda o: f"died on {clean(o)}",
        "nationality": lambda o: (
            "is an American"
            if clean(o) in ("United States", "USA", "U.S.", "U.S.A.")
            else f"is a {clean(o)} citizen"
        ),
        "servedAsChiefOfTheAstronautOfficeIn": lambda o: f"served as chief of the Astronaut Office in {clean(o)}",
        "title": lambda o: f"held the title \"{clean(o)}\"",
        "ribbonAward": lambda o: f"received the {clean(o)} ribbon",
        "award": lambda o: f"received the award {clean(o)}",
        "awards": lambda o: f"earned {clean(o)} awards",
        "deathPlace": lambda o: f"died in {clean(o)}",
    }

    # Templates for predicates where the subject is a mission (or other entity)
    # and the object is a person or organization.
    mission_phrases = {
        "operator": lambda s, o: f"{clean(s)} was operated by {clean(o)}",
        "backupPilot": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} was a backup pilot for {clean(s)}",
        "commander": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} was the commander of {clean(s)}",
        "crewMembers": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} was a crew member of {clean(s)}",
        "representative": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} was the representative of {clean(s)}",
    }

    # Group triples by subject.
    from collections import defaultdict
    grouped = defaultdict(list)
    for triple in triples:
        grouped[triple.subject].append(triple)

    # Build sentences per subject.
    sentences = []
    for subject, triples_for_subj in grouped.items():
        parts = []
        for triple in triples_for_subj:
            if triple.predicate in subj_phrases:
                parts.append(subj_phrases[triple.predicate](triple.object))
            elif triple.predicate in mission_phrases:
                parts.append(mission_phrases[triple.predicate](triple.subject, triple.object))
            else:
                parts.append(f"{triple.predicate} {triple.object}")
        # Join parts with commas, adding “and” before the last one if needed.
        if len(parts) == 1:
            clause = parts[0]
        else:
            clause = ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + ", and " + parts[-1]
        sentences.append(f"{subject} {clause}")

    # Combine all sentences into a paragraph.
    paragraph = ". ".join(sentences) + "."
    return paragraph[0].upper() + paragraph[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
